_help: Edit this file to change the quiz. 'correct' is the 1-based index of the right answer (1 = first option, 2 = second, 3 = third, 4 = fourth). 'options' can hold any number of choices (2 or more). Lines starting with an underscore are ignored by the quiz.
title: Revolution & Collapse

questions.question: Roughly how long was the Eastern Front battle line?
questions.explanation: The Eastern Front stretched over a thousand kilometres, from the Baltic Sea to the Black Sea — far longer than the Western Front.

questions.question: How did the Eastern Front differ from the Western Front in character?
options: It was a static trench war just like the West
It was mostly a naval campaign
It was vast and mobile — armies advanced and retreated across huge distances
It was largely fought from the air
questions.explanation: Unlike the deadlock of the West, the Eastern Front was vast and mobile, with no continuous wall of trenches.

questions.question: What was the state of the Russian army entering the war?
options: The smallest but best equipped army in Europe
The largest army in the world, but badly supplied
A volunteer force trained by the British
Numerically weak but with superior artillery
questions.explanation: Russia had the largest army in the world, but soldiers often lacked rifles, boots, and food. Poor leadership and weak industry produced massive casualties.

questions.question: When did the February Revolution take place (by the modern calendar)?
questions.explanation: Although called the 'February Revolution' under the old Russian calendar, the protests in Petrograd broke out in March 1917.

questions.question: What happened to Tsar Nicholas II during the February Revolution?
options: He abdicated, ending three hundred years of Romanov rule
He led the army against the protesters
questions.explanation: Within days of the protests, Nicholas II abdicated, ending 300 years of Romanov rule over Russia.

questions.question: What fatal decision did the Provisional Government make?
options: It immediately signed peace with Germany
It kept Russia in the war
It restored the Tsar to power
It declared war on Britain and France
questions.explanation: The Provisional Government kept Russia in the war — the fighting and suffering continued, and so did the people's anger.

questions.question: Who led the Bolsheviks?
questions.explanation: Vladimir Lenin led the Bolsheviks and seized power in November 1917.

questions.question: What was the simple, powerful Bolshevik slogan?
questions.explanation: Lenin's promise — 'Peace, Land, and Bread' — appealed to soldiers, peasants and workers exhausted by war and shortage.

questions.question: When did the Bolsheviks seize power (by the modern calendar)?
questions.explanation: Although known as the October Revolution under the old calendar, the Bolshevik seizure of power happened in November 1917.

questions.question: Which treaty took Russia out of the First World War?
questions.explanation: In March 1918 the Bolsheviks signed the Treaty of Brest-Litovsk with Germany and the Central Powers, ending Russia's war.

questions.question: Which territories did Russia surrender under Brest-Litovsk?
options: Ukraine, the Baltic states and Finland
Only a thin strip of land near Berlin
questions.explanation: Russia surrendered enormous territory — Ukraine, the Baltic states, and Finland — losing a huge share of its population, farmland, and industry.

questions.question: Roughly how many German divisions were transferred from the East to the Western Front after Russia's collapse?
options: None — the troops stayed in Russia
questions.explanation: Germany moved around fifty divisions — hundreds of thousands of soldiers — west, giving it for the first time a real numerical advantage.

questions.question: What was the name of Germany's massive 1918 attack in the West?
questions.explanation: In spring 1918 Germany launched the Kaiserschlacht — the 'Kaiser's Battle' — hoping to win before American troops arrived in force.

questions.question: Why did the Spring Offensive ultimately fail?
options: Germany ran out of ammunition before the first day
Russia rejoined the war on the Allied side
The army overstretched, supply lines failed, and fresh US troops arrived in France
Bad weather prevented any advance at all
questions.explanation: The offensive overstretched the German army; supply lines couldn't keep up, soldiers were exhausted, and fresh American troops were pouring into France.

questions.question: What was the long-term political significance of the Russian Revolution?
options: It produced the world's first communist state — the Soviet Union
It ended communism forever in Europe
It united Russia with Germany permanently
questions.explanation: The Revolution gave birth to the Soviet Union — the world's first communist state — which would shape global politics for the rest of the 20th century.
